name: Golden Onion and Zucchini Lasagne (Gourmet Mag 2/94)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds thinly sliced onions, about 6 cups
salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
1 1/2 cups freshly grated Parmesan, about 5 ounces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy skillet, melt 2 tablespoons butter.
2. Cook onions with dried thyme (if using dry), salt and pepper, covered, over moderately low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes, or until tender and pale golden. [I think this was no more than 15 minutes.]
3. Cook uncovered, stirring, for an additional 15 minutes. [5 minutes should be enough.]
4. Add wine and simmer until most of the wine has evaporated.
5. Stir in fresh thyme (if using fresh) and transfer to a bowl.
6. Preheat oven to 375°F.
7. Butter an 8-inch square baking dish.
8. In the cleaned skillet, melt 1 tablespoon butter.
9. Cook half of zucchini slices in one layer over moderate heat until tender, about 2 minutes on each side. Transfer to another bowl.
10. Cook remaining zucchini slices in the same manner.
11. In a small heavy saucepan, melt the remaining 3 tablespoons butter over moderately low heat.
12. Add flour and cook roux, whisking, for 3 minutes.
13. Add milk in a stream, whisking, and bring to a boil, whisking.
14. Add nutmeg, salt, and pepper to taste and simmer sauce until thickened, about 2 minutes.
15. Spread a few tablespoons of sauce on the bottom of the prepared dish.
16. Layer the ingredients in order from bottom to top:
- 1 square lasagne
- 1/2 onions
- 1/2 zucchini
- 1/3 sauce
- 1/3 Parmesan
- 1 square lasagne
- 1/2 onions
- 1/2 zucchini
- 1/3 sauce
- 1/3 Parmesan
- 1 square lasagne
- 1/3 sauce
- 1/3 Parmesan
17. Bake the lasagne in the middle of the oven for 25-30 minutes, or until golden. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
Serves 6-8 as a first course or side dish.
